questions.question: What is clustering?
options: b) Grouping similar unlabeled data points

questions.question: Which property describes how close points are inside one cluster?
Which family usually assumes clusters are roughly round and similar in size?
Which family can find curved or very irregular cluster shapes?
If data forms one ring of points inside another ring, which family is likely to struggle most?
What is the main advantage of hierarchical clustering?
options: a) It is always the fastest
b) You can choose different numbers of clusters later from the tree

questions.question: In density-based clustering, what usually happens to points in empty low-density regions?
options: b) They are labeled as noise
c) They join the nearest cluster
d) They are removed from the dataset

questions.question: In graph-based clustering, points are connected by edges when
options: a) they have the same color
b) their similarity is high enough
d) they have the same label

questions.question: High cohesion and high separation together indicate
You have groups of very different sizes and densities. Which family may require careful parameter tuning?
